name: Raman Spectroscopy
description: Raman spectroscopy detects inelastically scattered laser light: modes that change polarizability produce Stokes/anti-Stokes shifts after the intense Rayleigh line is filtered, giving a vibrational fingerprint that complements IR through the rule of mutual exclusion.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Sample + laser"]
    N2["Photons scatter"]
    N3{"Polarizability changes?"}
    N4["Inactive: only Rayleigh"]
    N5["Stokes / anti-Stokes shift"]
    N6["Inelastic scattering"]
    N7["Filter Rayleigh line"]
    N8["Record Raman shift"]
    N9["Complement IR modes"]
    N10["Vibrational fingerprint"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 -->|no| N4
    N4 --> N7
    N3 -->|yes| N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 --> N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 --> N9
    N9 --> N10

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: A new type of secondary radiation
sources.authors: Raman, C. V.; Krishnan, K. S.
